title: Asset Management Market Surges to $18,071 Billion by 2035 as AI, IoT, and Predictive Maintenance Transform Enterprise Operations | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global Asset Management Market is projected to grow from $885.92 billion in 2025 to $18,071 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 35.2%. Growth is driven by AI and IoT integration enabling predictive maintenance, with software dominating at 64% market share and cloud-based deployment leading. North America holds 39% market share, while the U.S. market is expected to grow from $96.69 billion to $677.89 billion by 2035.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: IBM Maximo platform is highlighted as a leading enterprise asset management solution managing physical equipment across manufacturing, energy, and infrastructure sectors, positioning the company to benefit from the 35.2% CAGR market growth.
Oracle EAM platform is mentioned as a major player in enterprise asset management, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding market driven by AI and IoT adoption.
Oracle EAM platform is mentioned as a major player in enterprise asset management, well-positioned to capture growth from the expanding market driven by AI and IoT adoption.

SAP PM platform is identified as a key enterprise asset management solution, positioned to benefit from the market's strong growth trajectory and increasing demand for predictive maintenance capabilities.
Listed among key players in the asset management market, positioned to benefit from cloud-based deployment dominance and digital transformation trends driving market expansion.
Identified as a key player in asset management solutions, well-positioned to capitalize on growth in manufacturing and industrial sectors driving the market's 35.2% CAGR.

Recent 2025 launch of ABB Ability SmartMaster for predictive maintenance demonstrates active innovation and market engagement, positioning the company to benefit from the growing adoption of AI-driven asset management solutions.

title: Mass Notification System Market by Software, Hardware, Application - Global Forecast to 2030
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global Mass Notification System (MNS) market is expected to grow from $28.88 billion in 2025 to $80.44 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 22.7%. Growth is driven by stringent regulatory requirements, cloud deployment acceleration, and the dominance of voice communication. North America leads the market while Asia Pacific shows the fastest growth. Key players are enhancing offerings with advanced technologies to meet compliance and safety mandates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Named as a key player enhancing MNS solutions, well-positioned to benefit from stringent regulatory demands and the shift toward cloud-based, scalable notification systems.
Listed among leading MNS vendors with opportunities to grow through cloud deployment acceleration and integration of real-time threat intelligence in unified communication platforms.
Identified as a key player in the MNS market, positioned to benefit from increased adoption across regulated sectors like healthcare, education, and transportation.
Listed as a key MNS provider with opportunities in building automation integration and compliance-aligned notification workflows across critical infrastructure sectors.
Included among key MNS market players, positioned to benefit from growing demand for secure, compliance-ready communication platforms in regulated industries.
Listed as a key player enhancing MNS offerings with new technologies to meet compliance and safety mandates, positioning the company to benefit from the 22.7% CAGR market growth.

title: Why a Tesla and SpaceX Merger Would Not Be Great News for Tesla Shareholders
author: Lee Samaha
description: While a potential Tesla-SpaceX merger could offer benefits like securing chip supply chains through their Terafab joint venture, the article argues it would be detrimental to Tesla shareholders. A merger would dilute Tesla's value precisely as the company is poised to scale major initiatives like robotaxis and Optimus robots in 2026-2027. The trend in industrial sectors favors focused companies over conglomerates, and Tesla can fund its own growth without relying on SpaceX.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Tesla has strong growth prospects with robotaxi and Optimus scaling in 2026-2027, but a SpaceX merger would be negative for shareholders by diluting value at a critical moment. The company itself is fundamentally sound but the merger proposal is unfavorable.

Mentioned as an example of a large industrial company that has split up, reflecting investor preference for focused companies over conglomerates. Used as a comparative reference point, not subject to direct analysis.
Cited as another example of industrial sector trend toward company breakups and focused operations. Used as comparative reference, not directly analyzed.

title: Enterprise Software Leaders Build AI Agents With NVIDIA
author: Nvidia
description: NVIDIA announced new Agent Toolkit software including NemoClaw blueprints, Nemotron 3 Ultra models, and OpenShell secure runtime to enable enterprises to build autonomous AI agents. Major partners like Cadence, Dassault Systèmes, Siemens, Synopsys, CrowdStrike, and Palantir are deploying these agents for engineering workflows, cybersecurity, and operations. NVIDIA also partnered with Microsoft, Canonical, and Red Hat to integrate OpenShell across Windows, Linux, and enterprise platforms.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: NVIDIA is launching comprehensive new AI agent software and models with strong enterprise adoption from leading software companies, positioning itself as the foundational platform for enterprise AI agent development.
Cadence is among the first to deploy NVIDIA NemoClaw for autonomous chip design and verification, with its ChipStack AI Super Agent compressing weeks of engineering work into hours.
Dassault is productizing its 3DEXPERIENCE platform with NVIDIA NemoClaw and OpenShell for long-running autonomous agents across design, simulation, and manufacturing.
Siemens is integrating NVIDIA NemoClaw and OpenShell into its Fuse EDA AI Agent for autonomous semiconductor and circuit board design workflows.
Synopsys is collaborating with NVIDIA to build autonomous AI engineers for chip design with focus on achieving full workflow autonomy.
CrowdStrike is deploying NVIDIA Nemotron models for specialized cybersecurity agents that continuously identify and remediate vulnerabilities, enhancing operational efficiency.
Palantir is integrating NVIDIA Nemotron models into its AI FDE platform to autonomously execute complex tasks and build domain-specific enterprise systems.
Microsoft is collaborating with NVIDIA to deliver native Windows experience for personal agents with new security primitives and OpenShell integration.

title: Digital Twin Market Expected to Reach US$ 140 Billion by 2031
author: The Insight Partners
description: The global digital twin market, valued at $13.74 billion in 2024, is expected to grow at a 40.1% CAGR to reach $140.94 billion by 2031. Growth is driven by increasing IoT adoption, Industry 4.0 implementation, and enterprise focus on predictive maintenance. North America leads with 35% market share, while Asia Pacific is the fastest-growing region. Large enterprises and healthcare sectors show the strongest adoption rates.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a major cloud and AI provider offering Azure Digital Twins platform; recent development with Kyndryl announced in April 2026 for AI-powered workplace digital twin solutions
Leading provider of virtual twin experiences through 3DEXPERIENCE platform for aerospace, automotive, and life sciences sectors
Industrial software company specializing in IoT-enabled digital twin and PLM solutions; announced expansion of NVIDIA collaboration in July 2025 to accelerate design and simulation capabilities
Enterprise AI and hybrid cloud provider delivering digital twin solutions for asset optimization and predictive maintenance
Cloud infrastructure and enterprise software provider supporting digital twin applications in supply chain and smart operations
Cloud infrastructure and enterprise software provider supporting digital twin applications in supply chain and smart operations
Design and engineering software company supporting digital twin adoption in architecture, construction, and infrastructure projects
Identified as a global industrial automation leader with comprehensive digital twin capabilities across manufacturing, energy, and smart infrastructure
